content:
# Acts Chapter 3 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 227-228 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/ACT_003_page_001.jpg, ACT_003_page_002.jpg

---

**1** Now Peter and John were-going-up into the temple upon the hour of-the prayer, the ninth [hour].

**2** And a certain man, being lame from [the] womb of-[his] mother, was-being-carried, whom they-were-placing each day at the gate of-the temple, the [one] being-called Beautiful, to-ask alms from the [ones] going-in into the temple;

**3** who, having-seen Peter and John about to-go-in into the temple, was-asking to-receive alms.

**4** And Peter, having-gazed-at him, together-with John, said, "Look at us."

**5** And he was-fixing-attention-on them, expecting to-receive something from them.

**6** But Peter said, "Silver and gold is not belonging to-me, but what I-have, this I-give to-you: in the name of-Jesus Christ the Nazarene, walk."

**7** And having-seized him [by] the right hand, he-raised [him] up; and immediately his feet and ankles were-made-firm.

**8** And leaping-up, he-stood and was-walking, and he-entered together-with them into the temple, walking and leaping and praising God.

**9** And all the people saw him walking and praising God;

**10** and they-were-recognizing him, that this-one was the [one] toward the alms sitting at the Beautiful Gate of-the temple; and they-were-filled-with astonishment and amazement at the [thing] having-happened to-him.

**11** And [as] he was-holding-fast Peter and John, all the people ran-together toward them upon the porch, the [one] being-called of-Solomon, utterly-astonished.

**12** And having-seen [this], Peter answered toward the people, "Men, Israelites, why are-you-marveling at this, or why to-us are-you-gazing-intently, as-if by-own power or godliness having-made him to-walk?

**13** The God of-Abraham and of-Isaac and of-Jacob, the God of-the fathers of-us, glorified his servant Jesus, whom you delivered-over and denied before [the] face of-Pilate, that-one having-judged to-release [him].

**14** But you denied the Holy and Righteous [One], and asked-for a man, a murderer, to-be-granted to-you,

**15** but the originator of-the life you-killed, whom God raised out-of [the] dead, of-which we are witnesses.

**16** And upon the faith of-the name of-him, this [man] whom you-behold and you-know, his name made-firm, and the faith the [one] through him gave to-him this complete-soundness before all of-you.

**17** And now, brothers, I-know that according-to ignorance you-acted, as also the rulers of-you.

**18** But God, what-things he-proclaimed-before through [the] mouth of-all the prophets, [that] the Christ of-him [was] to-suffer, he-fulfilled thus.

**19** Repent therefore and turn-back, unto the being-wiped-out of-the sins of-you,

**20** so-that times of-refreshing might-come from [the] face of-the Lord, and he-might-send the [one] having-been-appointed-before for-you, Christ Jesus,

**21** whom it-is-necessary [for] heaven indeed to-receive until [the] times of-restoration of-all-things, of-which God spoke through [the] mouth of-the holy prophets of-him from [the] age.

**22** Moses indeed said that, "A prophet the Lord the God of-you will-raise-up for-you out-of the brothers of-you as me; him you-shall-hear according-to all-things whatever he-might-speak to-you.

**23** And it-shall-be, every soul which might not hear that prophet will-be-utterly-destroyed out-of the people."

**24** And all the prophets also, from Samuel and the [ones] in-succession, as-many-as spoke, also proclaimed-before these days.

**25** You are the sons of-the prophets and of-the covenant which God covenanted toward the fathers of-you, saying toward Abraham, "And in the seed of-you shall-be-blessed all the families of-the earth."

**26** To-you first, God, having-raised-up his servant, sent him blessing you, in the turning-away [of] each [one] from the wickednesses of-you.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 6 -- "the Nazarene" (ΝΑΖΩΡΑΙΟΥ):** Sinaiticus reads ΝΑΖΩΡΑΙΟΥ ("Nazarene/Nazoraean"). Some later manuscripts read ΝΑΖΑΡΗΝΟΥ. The Sinaiticus form is preserved here.

2. **Verse 11 -- "holding-fast" (ΚΡΑΤΟΥΝΤΟΣ):** Sinaiticus reads the genitive singular participle ΚΡΑΤΟΥΝΤΟΣ with "him" (αυτου) as subject -- i.e., the healed man holding fast to Peter and John. The construction is genitive absolute.

3. **Verse 13 -- "servant" (ΠΑΙΔΑ):** Greek ΠΑΙΣ can mean "child" or "servant." In this Isaianic-allusion context (cf. Isaiah 52:13 LXX), "servant" is the more precise rendering. Preserved literally.

4. **Verse 15 -- "originator" (ΑΡΧΗΓΟΝ):** ΑΡΧΗΓΟΣ carries the sense of "author," "pioneer," "originator," or "prince." Rendered "originator" to preserve the literal force -- the one who originates or leads-forth life. Other witnesses agree.

5. **Verse 16 -- Double faith construction:** The verse contains an unusual doubled reference to faith (ΕΠΙ ΤΗ ΠΙΣΤΕΙ ... Η ΠΙΣΤΙΣ Η ΔΙ ΑΥΤΟΥ). This awkward construction is preserved as-is; it is not a scribal error but reflects the Greek emphasis.

6. **Verse 20 -- "having-been-appointed-before" (ΠΡΟΚΕΧΕΙΡΙΣΜΕΝΟΝ):** Sinaiticus reads ΠΡΟΚΕΧΕΙΡΙΣΜΕΝΟΝ (perfect passive participle of ΠΡΟΧΕΙΡΙΖΩ, "to appoint/designate beforehand"). Some manuscripts read ΠΡΟΚΕΚΗΡΥΓΜΕΝΟΝ ("having been proclaimed before"). The Sinaiticus reading is followed.

7. **Verse 21 -- "from the age" (ΑΠ ΑΙΩΝΟΣ):** Literally "from [the] age," meaning "from of old" or "from antiquity." Preserved in its literal awkwardness.

8. **Verse 22 -- Deuteronomy 18:15-16 citation:** The quotation follows the LXX form closely. Sinaiticus includes "the Lord the God of you" (ΚΥΡΙΟΣ Ο ΘΕΟΣ ΥΜΩΝ) rather than shorter variants found in some witnesses.

9. **Verse 23 -- "utterly-destroyed" (ΕΞΟΛΕΘΡΕΥΘΗΣΕΤΑΙ):** A compound verb (ΕΞ + ΟΛΕΘΡΕΥΩ) intensifying the destruction. The prefix ΕΞ- ("out of/utterly") is preserved via the hyphenated rendering. This echoes LXX Deuteronomy 18:19.

10. **Verse 26 -- "having-raised-up" (ΑΝΑΣΤΗΣΑΣ):** This verb (ΑΝΙΣΤΗΜΙ) can mean "raised up" in the sense of "brought onto the scene" or "raised from the dead." In context, paralleling v. 22 (God raising up a prophet), the sense is likely "having raised up [as servant/prophet]" rather than resurrection, though the ambiguity may be intentional. The literal form is preserved without interpretive smoothing.

-- EMET
